ddls:
CREATE TABLE "security_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "time_generated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the event was generated (UTC) */,
  "device_vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the device that generated the log */,
  "device_product" TEXT /* Product related to the device */,
  "event_class_id" TEXT /* Class ID assigned to the type of event */,
  "log_severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the log event */,
  "message" TEXT /* Descriptive message of the event */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source device */,
  "device_version" TEXT /* Version of the device software */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Description of the activity that was detected */,
  "file_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the file associated with the event */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file related to the event */,
  "source_host_name" TEXT /* Hostname of the source device */,
  "source_user_name" TEXT /* Username of the account that triggered the event */,
  "additional_extensions" TEXT /* Additional context or extensions related to the event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of security event (e.g., network, endpoint, application) */,
  "event_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the log_sources table */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the attack_types table */,
  "confidence_level" TEXT /* Confidence level of the event detection */,
  "geolocation" TEXT /* Geolocation data for the source IP */,
  "mitre_tactic_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework tactics */,
  "mitre_technique_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework techniques */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_mitre_technique_id FOREIGN KEY ("mitre_technique_id") REFERENCES attack_types ("mitre_technique_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_event_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_source_id") REFERENCES log_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_source_user_name FOREIGN KEY ("source_user_name") REFERENCES users ("username"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_device_vendor FOREIGN KEY ("device_vendor") REFERENCES devices ("device_vendor"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_types ("attack_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_mitre_tactic_id FOREIGN KEY ("mitre_tactic_id") REFERENCES attack_types ("mitre_tactic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the device */,
  "device_product" TEXT /* Product name of the device */,
  "device_version" TEXT /* Version of the device's software */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., firewall, endpoint, server) */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Last time the device was seen active */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the device */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last successful login */,
  "failed_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  "locked" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is locked */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or array of user permissions */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response action */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the security event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who took the action */,
  "response_action" TEXT /* Description of the response action */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response action was taken */,
  "response_template_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the response_templates table */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "response_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the response action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_responses_response_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_template_id") REFERENCES response_templates ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_responses_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_responses_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attack_types" (
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attack type */,
  "attack_name" TEXT /* Name of the attack */,
  "attack_category" TEXT /* Category of the attack (e.g., FILELESS, TOOL) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the attack */,
  "mitre_tactic_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework tactics */,
  "mitre_technique_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework techniques */,
  "tactic_description" TEXT /* Description of the associated MITRE tactic */,
  "technique_description" TEXT /* Description of the associated MITRE technique */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_severities" (
  "severity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each severity level */,
  "severity_name" TEXT /* Name of the severity (e.g., Info, Warning, Critical) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the severity level */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code for severity levels */,
  "alert_threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold for generating alerts based on severity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the event class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event class */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., network, endpoint, application) */,
  "event_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the log_sources table */,
  "event_severity_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the log_severities table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the log source (e.g., Firewall, Endpoint) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of log source (e.g., firewall, endpoint, application) */,
  "source_status" TEXT /* Status of the log source (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last time the log source was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related security event */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" INTEGER /* Severity of the alert */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., network, endpoint, application) */,
  "alert_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the log_sources table */,
  "alert_confidence_level" TEXT /* Confidence level of the alert detection */,
  "mitre_tactic_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework tactics */,
  "mitre_technique_id" INTEGER /* ID linking to the MITRE ATT&CK framework techniques */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_mitre_technique_id FOREIGN KEY ("mitre_technique_id") REFERENCES attack_types ("mitre_technique_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_mitre_tactic_id FOREIGN KEY ("mitre_tactic_id") REFERENCES attack_types ("mitre_tactic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_alert_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_source_id") REFERENCES log_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response template */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the response template */,
  "template_description" TEXT /* Description of the actions included in the template */,
  "template_type" TEXT /* Type of response template (e.g., block, investigate, notify) */,
  "template_status" TEXT /* Status of the template (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "template_severity_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the log_severities table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client performing the action */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the action was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forensics" (
  "forensic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for forensic data entry */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related security event */,
  "analysis_data" TEXT /* Details of the forensic analysis */,
  "analysis_tool" TEXT /* Tool used for the forensic analysis */,
  "analysis_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the analysis was performed */,
  "forensic_tool_version" TEXT /* Version of the forensic tool used */,
  "forensic_status" TEXT /* Status of the forensic analysis (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  "forensic_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the forensic analysis */,
  "forensic_evidence" TEXT /* JSON object or array of evidence collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forensic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forensics_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id")
)
